summary: Renamed Baileys fork silently forces installer's WhatsApp account to follow author-controlled newsletter channels
details: @budetzz/baileys is a fork of the WhatsApp library Baileys that declares the upstream project's GitHub URL as its own homepage and remaps the libsignal dependency to an author-controlled alias (npm:@budetzz/libsignal-node). When a consumer calls makeWASocket(), lib/Socket/newsletter.js sets a 90-second setTimeout that fetches a JSON list of newsletter JIDs from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/budetzz/mazzbudetzzzzz/refs/heads/main/saluran.json and issues an authenticated FOLLOW WMex query for each JID using the installer's logged-in WhatsApp identity. The destination list is mutable and remote-controlled, the action is undocumented, and the WhatsApp account performing the FOLLOW is the consumer's, not the author's. Separately, lib/index.js performs an unconditional top-level fetch on every require() to https://raw.githubusercontent.com/z4phdev/client/refs/heads/main/information.json (currently only console.log'd, but a persistent author->installer channel under a mutable personal-user repo). The combination — upstream-impersonating fork + silent account-level action against attacker-curated targets via the consumer's authenticated session + import-time beacon to a second mutable personal repo — is silent-relay of installer-side WhatsApp identity to author-controlled destinations.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
